title: Charitable giving
description: Practice understanding of charitable giving and why it matters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving provides many benefits, some of which are not even financial in nature. 

**What is the *happiness paradox* that relates to charitable giving?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The more you give, the more you receive.
The more you have, the less you need.
The more you spend, the less you save.
The more you share, the less you have.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving provides many benefits, some of which are not even financial in nature. 

**What is the *warm glow effect* that is related to charitable giving?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The satisfaction people feel when they know they are helping others.
The response people have when they are in a warm environment.
The tendency for people to become more giving when they are in a good mood.
The feeling people get when they receive a tax deduction for their donation.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving provides many benefits, some of which are not even financial in nature. 

**What is the *gratitude effect* that is related to charitable giving?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when individuals feel grateful for what they have
when individuals feel appreciated for giving to charity
when individuals feel a sense of connection and community with those they are helping
when people are grateful to receive a tax deduction for their donation

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving does not always involve donating money. 

**How can you give to a charity without using money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: by helping out at a charity's event or project
by shopping at a charity's store or website
by asking a charity for a scholarship or a grant
by avoiding or complaining about a charity

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving does not always involve donating money. 

**How can you give to a charity without using money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: by donating items
by shopping at a charity's store or website
by asking a charity for a scholarship or a grant
by avoiding or complaining about a charity

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving does not always involve donating money. 

**How can you give to a charity without using money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: by offering your services
by shopping at a charity's store or website
by asking a charity for a scholarship or a grant
by avoiding or complaining about a charity

assessment_items.question_content:
Your employer gives you $\$100$ to donate to a *charity* of your choice. 

**Who qualifies as an actual charity for your donation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a GoFundMe campaign for your friend's cousin who needs surgery
a political party that you support in the upcoming election
a health organization that researches and treats a disease that affects your family
a church in your neighborhood

assessment_items.question_content:
Your employer gives you $\$200$ to donate to a *charity* of your choice. 

**Who qualifies as an actual charity for your donation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a GoFundMe campaign for a person whose house burned down
a political party that you support in the upcoming election
World Wildlife Fund that works to protect endangered animals and habitats around the world.
a synagogue in your town

assessment_items.question_content:
Your employer gives you $\$200$ to donate to a *charity* of your choice. 

**Who qualifies as an actual charity for your donation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a local arts and culture organization
a friend who is accepting donations to rebuild her home after a fire
Habitat for Humanity that builds homes for low-income families around the world
a local mosque

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving not only has a positive impact on the causes you support, but it can also have financial benefits for you.

**Which of the following is a benefit of charitable giving?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It can lower the amount of income you have to pay taxes on.
It can increase your salary.
It lowers your tax refund.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving not only has a positive impact on the causes you support, but it can also have financial benefits for you.

**Which of the following is a benefit of charitable giving?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It can increase your tax deductions or credits.
It increases how much you owe in taxes.
It lowers your tax refund.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charitable giving not only has a positive impact on the causes you support, but it can also have financial benefits for you.

**Which of the following is a benefit of charitable giving?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Donations can lower estate taxes.
Charitable organizations will pay the taxes on the estate, if you were to pass away.
You can buy any property tax-free.
